response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack engineer with impressive breadth across web, mobile, and distributed systems, but he is fundamentally mismatched with this role. The position requires native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX, and hands-on Linux kernel source modification for embedded LAN-switch security features; the candidate lists no Japanese at all, his C/C++ experience is a brief stint over a decade ago, and his career is centered on TypeScript/Node.js product development with no evidence of embedded networking, switch platforms, or security technologies like MACsec or 802.1X.
strengths: Long, progressively senior engineering career including staff-level roles at major technology companies
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems (async I/O, caching, fibers in DLang) and comfort working on Linux
Some systems-adjacent background: C/C++ components at National Instruments, robotic-arm tooling, and hardware/audio projects
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, which is a hard requirement (native-level) for this role
No demonstrated embedded networking, LAN-switch, Linux kernel modification, or security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption) experience
C programming experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014–2015); recent work is almost entirely web/full-stack TypeScript

key_factors.factor: No embedded C / Linux kernel / network-security background
Strong general seniority and systems-programming aptitude
